fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer dietitian license to new jersey, dietitian compact new jersey, new jersey dietitian endorsement application

board.name: State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: State fees have not yet been finalized. CDR exam fee is $200 nationally.

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (Pending)
license_types.description: New Jersey enacted a law in 2020 requiring dietitian and nutritionist licensure. The Board of Dietetics and Nutrition is actively drafting regulations. No state licenses are currently being issued.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the CDR Registration Examination for Dietitians. Active CDR registration will automatically qualify individuals for NJ state licensure once the process opens.

steps.title: Monitor the Board for Application Opening
steps.description: The NJ Board of Dietetics and Nutrition is holding public sessions (e.g., February 5, 2026) to finalize regulations. Check the NJ Consumer Affairs website for when applications begin.

steps.title: Submit Application (When Available)
steps.description: Once regulations are adopted, submit a written application with the required fee to the Board.

documents.detail: Active CDR registration as an RD/RDN will be the primary qualifying credential.
Application materials will be released by the Board once regulations are finalized.

provisional_text: New Jersey's regulations have not yet been finalized. No temporary or provisional licenses are currently being issued.
reciprocity_text: Reciprocity provisions will be defined in the final regulations. Active CDR registration is expected to serve as the primary qualifying credential for licensure by endorsement.

faqs.question: Is New Jersey currently issuing dietitian licenses?
faqs.answer: No. Although NJ enacted a licensure law in 2020, the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition is still drafting the implementing regulations. Licenses will be issued once these regulations are officially adopted.

faqs.question: When will New Jersey start accepting license applications?
faqs.answer: No firm date has been announced. The Board is holding public sessions to finalize regulations. Monitor the NJ Division of Consumer Affairs website for updates.

faqs.question: Can I practice as a dietitian in New Jersey now?
faqs.answer: Yes. Until the regulations are fully enacted, practicing with a CDR credential (RD/RDN) in New Jersey remains the standard. Employers generally require active CDR registration.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take TBD (Regulations Pending). If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Reciprocity provisions will be defined in the final regulations. Active CDR registration is expected to serve as the primary qualifying credential for licensure by endorsement.
